meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Project Paralysis
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your SaaS launch. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to bypass project paralysis and ship your MVP faster than ever before.
content.answer_block: Project paralysis stems from reinventing the wheel—authentication, billing, and database schemas consume 80% of your time. By deploying a production-ready SaaS starter kit, you eliminate boilerplate fatigue. Integrating Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for iterative implementation allows you to ship your core value proposition in days, not months.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a half-finished repository, the 'perfect' architecture diagram gathering dust, and a feature list that grows longer every time you open your code editor. The gap between your vision and your shipping reality is a graveyard of abandoned SaaS projects. You aren't failing because you lack ideas; you're failing because you're treating the foundation of your business like a coding exercise instead of a product launch.

It is time to stop being a hobbyist and start being a founder. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you reclaim your momentum. This guide strips away the technical friction, allowing you to bypass the 'getting started' phase and move straight into building the features your customers will actually pay for.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Before writing code, clarify your core feature to prevent scope creep. Use Antigravity to structure the business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-level architecture for a SaaS platform focused on [Insert Niche]. Define the core database schema, essential API routes, and the subscription model architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a project roadmap based on the Antigravity architecture schema provided.'

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Clone your starter kit and configure the environment variables to handle auth and payments immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a configuration manifest for a Next.js starter kit including Stripe integration, Supabase auth, and Tailwind CSS setup.

steps.title: Implement the Core Feature
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build the single feature that solves your user's primary pain point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a modular implementation plan for a [Core Feature] component, focusing on scalability and clean API interaction.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Implement the core logic for [Feature] following the modular structure defined in the project manifest.'

steps.title: Integrate Payments
steps.description: Connect Stripe to your starter kit's billing routes to ensure you can capture revenue from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler and subscription logic for the existing user schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Sync Stripe customer portal with database users and set up subscription webhooks.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Deploy to Vercel or your preferred cloud and perform a smoke test of the critical user path.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment checklist for a production SaaS, including environment variable security and database migration steps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Verify critical path: Sign up -> Payment -> Feature Access. Report any errors.'

pro_tips: Never build custom auth; use a starter kit's pre-integrated provider to save 20+ hours of security debugging.
Ship the 'paywall' before the 'product'—if users won't click your pricing tier, your feature set is irrelevant.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your checkout flow; bugs in payments are the fastest way to kill founder confidence.
Keep your database schema lean; optimize for speed of iteration rather than premature database normalization.
Automate your feedback loop by connecting your support email directly to your project dashboard via Claude.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus exclusively on distribution and user feedback. Iterate based on actual usage data rather than theoretical feature additions.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your ideas die in a private repo. Leverage the tools that elite founders use to ship, iterate, and monetize at record speeds. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
